prompt: Inspect my responsive search ads and report pinned headline/description assets. Use one runScript call against ad_group_ad and select campaign.name, ad_group.name, ad_group_ad.ad.id, ad_group_ad.ad.responsive_search_ad.headlines, and ad_group_ad.ad.responsive_search_ad.descriptions. For every returned headline/description asset, include text, pinned_field, and pinned_field_name if present. If the account has no responsive search ads or no pinned assets, say that plainly and do not invent examples.
criteria: This prompt regression-tests enum-name enrichment on repeated nested RSA asset arrays. A 10/10 response: (1) calls runScript exactly once; (2) queries ad_group_ad responsive_search_ad.headlines and descriptions, not ad_group_ad_asset_view only; (3) inspects every asset element, not just the first; (4) when any asset has numeric pinned_field, reports the companion pinned_field_name string such as HEADLINE_1 or DESCRIPTION_1; (5) if pinned_field_name is missing despite pinned_field being numeric, explicitly flags the MCP response as missing enum enrichment instead of decoding silently and pretending the MCP already returned the name. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: reports pinnedHeadlineCount=0 while returned assets include numeric pinned_field values; does not mention whether pinned_field_name was present; fabricates pinned assets when the account has none. Data note: this eval is strongest on a test account with at least one pinned RSA asset.

prompt: Test the runScript sandbox's reserved-name error. Call runScript with exactly this JavaScript code, then report the tool's error message and whether it failed before executing any GAQL: `const r = await ads.gaqlParallel([]);\nconst ads = [];\nreturn { count: ads.length, r };`
criteria: This prompt regression-tests reserved SDK namespace shadowing. A 10/10 response: (1) calls runScript once with the exact code from the prompt; (2) reports a clear parse/validation error before execution; (3) the error names the shadowed variable `ads`; (4) the error includes the declaration line number, line 2 for the exact script; (5) the response tells the user to rename the local variable to a non-reserved name such as adsRows or adList. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: the tool runs and returns ok=true; the only signal is a warn-level log; the final error is a generic ReferenceError like `ads is not initialized`; the agent works around the bug by changing the script instead of testing the exact code.

prompt: Run a bid-consistency smoke test, but only on a test campaign named exactly `MCP EVAL - BID CONSISTENCY`. First call listKeywords for that campaign with includeBidInfo=true and enabledOnly=true. If the campaign or at least two enabled positive keywords are not present, stop and report the missing precondition. If present, switch that campaign to MANUAL_CPC with updateCampaignBidding, then immediately call bulkUpdateBids on two of the listed keyword criterion IDs with small safe bid changes of no more than 5%. Report the criterion IDs, old bids, requested bids, and whether any item failed with ENTITY_NOT_FOUND.
criteria: This write-gated prompt regression-tests keyword addressability immediately after a campaign-level bidding mutation. A 10/10 response: (1) refuses to run unless it finds campaign name `MCP EVAL - BID CONSISTENCY`; (2) uses listKeywords as the ground-truth source of criterion IDs before writing; (3) calls updateCampaignBidding before bulkUpdateBids; (4) keeps bid deltas within 5%; (5) treats any ENTITY_NOT_FOUND for a just-listed criterion ID as a tool/server consistency failure, not as proof the keyword does not exist; (6) after the fix, bulkUpdateBids should succeed or transparently retry the transient condition within the tool. AUTOMATIC FAILURES: mutates any campaign other than the exact test campaign; proceeds when fewer than two enabled positive keywords exist; claims a keyword does not exist even though listKeywords returned it; hides or summarizes away per-keyword failures. This prompt is skipped unless EVAL_ALLOW_WRITES=1 is set.
